name: CMA-SST
citation: Chen, L., Cao, L., Zhou, Z., Zhang, D., and Liao, J. (2021). A new globally reconstructed sea surface temperature analysis dataset since 1900. Journal of Meteorological Research, 35, 911-925. https://doi.org/10.1007/s13351-021-1098-7.

Chen, L., Xu, W., Zhou, Z. et al. A new global land-ocean merged surface temperature dataset since the 1850s: the CMA-GMST dataset. Climate Dynamics, 63, 187 (2025). https://doi.org/10.1007/s00382-025-07614-x.
data_citation: China Meteorological Administration. China Meteorological Administration Global Merged Surface Temperature (CMA-GMST). https://data.cma.cn/en/#/Visualization/Visualization-detail?id=16.
China Meteorological Administration. SDK Download Guidance. https://data.cma.cn/en/#/Visualization/cra-api.
acknowledgement: CMA-SST/CMA-GMST data were obtained from the China Meteorological Administration data service. Record the exact access date and source file used during processing.
notes: CMA product id 16 is documented by the CMA portal as CMA-GMST, a land-ocean merged gridded historical surface-temperature anomaly product. The API fetcher requires local CMDCapi.py and CMA_USER_ID. The authenticated API currently returns yearly ZIP files containing monthly 2-degree NetCDF grids, for example SURF_CLI_GLB_MST_MON_GRID_2DEG_185301.nc, not a single global monthly anomaly CSV. The CMA API fetcher writes CMA-SST_Global_Month_Temp_1981_2010.csv only after inspecting the NetCDF schema, identifying the gridded anomaly variable, parsing complete monthly year-month coverage, and passing cosine-latitude area-weighted global mean QC. The generated CMA series still requires reference reconciliation because the current CMA API grids do not numerically match the reference precomputed CMA-SST CSV.
